text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY CLINIC -- ON-STUDY PROTOCOL VISIT NOTE
Phase II Clinical Trial: Savolitinib + Osimertinib for MET-Amplified EGFR-Mutant NSCLC Post-Third-Generation TKI Progression
Visit Type: 6-Month Protocol Restaging Visit / On-Trial Oncology Assessment

---

ATTENDING ONCOLOGIST: [Attending Physician]
FELLOW/APP: [Fellow/Advanced Practice Provider]
DEPARTMENT: Thoracic Medical Oncology

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT

Ms. Margaret Yuen is a 65-year-old woman with Stage IV (M1b, brain metastases) EGFR L858R-mutant non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, currently enrolled on a Phase II investigator-sponsored clinical trial of savolitinib 300 mg orally once daily in combination with osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg orally once daily for MET-amplified NSCLC following progression on third-generation EGFR TKI therapy. She presents today for her 6-month on-protocol restaging visit, assessment of tolerability, and treatment continuation discussion. Protocol-specified restaging imaging (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast and MRI brain with and without gadolinium) has been completed and reviewed prior to today's visit.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Yuen has a well-established oncologic history spanning approximately two years. In brief, she was originally diagnosed with Stage IIIB right upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma harboring an EGFR L858R mutation (exon 21), with N3 nodal involvement (right supraclavicular and contralateral mediastinal nodes) and no evidence of distant metastases at diagnosis. Given the presence of a targetable EGFR driver mutation and her stage precluding surgical resection, she was initiated on osimertinib monotherapy as first-line therapy, achieving a significant partial response with reduction of her primary mass from 5.8 cm to approximately 1.8 cm, and near-complete nodal resolution. She had a durable response on osimertinib lasting approximately 14 months.

She then developed intracranial progression with three new brain metastases (left frontal 12 mm, right cerebellar 8 mm, left parietal 6 mm) and concurrent systemic progression (primary tumor enlargement from 1.8 to 2.7 cm, new right paratracheal node). Liquid biopsy (Guardant360) at that time identified an acquired EGFR T790M resistance mutation at low allele frequency (0.6%), and subsequent tissue re-biopsy with FoundationOne CDx profiling revealed a complex, heterogeneous resistance landscape: low-level T790M (3.1% AF), high-level MET amplification (copy number ratio 5.1, FISH-confirmed), and newly acquired PIK3CA p.E545K and PTEN p.R130Q alterations. She underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife) to all three brain metastases with good tolerability, received four cycles of bridging carboplatin/pemetrexed with documented partial response in the chest, and was subsequently enrolled on the present Phase II trial targeting the dominant MET-amplified resistance mechanism.

She initiated savolitinib plus osimertinib approximately six months ago. At the 8-week on-trial restaging, she demonstrated a partial response with the right upper lobe mass decreasing from 2.1 cm (pre-trial) to 1.4 cm, the right paratracheal node becoming unmeasurable, and favorable intracranial responses (left frontal down to 4 mm, left parietal no longer visualized). The molecular correlative data from that timepoint showed declining ctDNA (EGFR L858R allele frequency 0.3%), and MET amplification falling below detection threshold in peripheral blood, both of which were encouraging indicators of biologic activity.

Today she presents for 6-month protocol restaging. She reports that overall she is doing reasonably well. Her major complaints are mild, persistent fatigue -- which she rates as 3/10 in severity -- and some difficulty concentrating at work, which she attributes partly to fatigue and partly to having resumed teaching part-time. She has no new headaches, no focal neurological symptoms, no visual changes, no seizures. Her lower extremity edema, which was a grade 2 issue early in the trial related to savolitinib, has remained well-controlled on furosemide 20 mg daily. She has no new dyspnea beyond mild exertional breathlessness with steep stairs, which she says is unchanged from prior visits. She denies chest pain, hemoptysis, or new cough. She has had no fever, chills, or signs of infection. Appetite remains slightly diminished compared to her baseline prior to her cancer diagnosis, but she has maintained her weight over the past several months without significant loss. She denies nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea. She notes a mild, intermittent facial rash (grade 1, primarily over the forehead and nasal bridge) consistent with the ongoing EGFR TKI effect from osimertinib, which she manages with topical moisturizer and intermittent topical clindamycin. She finds this cosmetically tolerable.

She completed her dexamethasone taper months ago without neurological deterioration and is not on any systemic corticosteroids at this time. She is managing her other medications without difficulty.

Of note, she has returned to part-time teaching (3 days per week) and reports that this has been personally meaningful and restorative. She continues to reside with her family. She feels emotionally supported.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: Mild fatigue (3/10). No fever. No chills. No significant unintentional weight loss (weight stable x 3 months). Mild appetite decrease, stable.
Neurological: No headaches. No focal weakness. No seizures. No vision changes. No word-finding difficulty (resolved). Mild concentration difficulties attributed to fatigue and return to work.
Pulmonary: Mild exertional dyspnea with steep inclines, unchanged from prior. No hemoptysis. No new cough. No pleuritic chest pain.
Cardiovascular: Bilateral lower extremity edema, grade 1, currently controlled on furosemide. No palpitations, no orthopnea, no PND. Echocardiogram obtained at time of edema onset confirmed normal LV function (EF 60-65%).
Dermatologic: Grade 1 acneiform/papulopustular rash over forehead and nasal bridge. Paronychia resolved. No new skin lesions.
Gastrointestinal: Mild nausea intermittently, grade 1. No vomiting. No diarrhea. No constipation. No abdominal pain.
Musculoskeletal: No new bone pain. No joint pain.
Endocrine/Metabolic: No symptoms of hyperglycemia or adrenal insufficiency.
Hematologic: No bruising, no bleeding, no known infections.
All other systems reviewed and negative.

---

PAST MEDICAL AND ONCOLOGIC HISTORY
(As previously documented; summarized for protocol visit note)

1. Stage IV (M1b) non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe, EGFR L858R-mutant, MET-amplified at resistance -- currently on Phase II trial (savolitinib + osimertinib)
   - Original staging: Stage IIIB (T3 N3 M0), diagnosed approximately 2 years ago
   - Progression to Stage IV with brain metastases at approximately 14 months on osimertinib
   - Status post SRS x3 brain metastases (Gamma Knife)
   - Status post 4 cycles carboplatin/pemetrexed (bridging therapy prior to trial)
2. Small vessel ischemic disease (incidental, periventricular T2/FLAIR changes on MRI brain)
3. Former smoker: 12 pack-year history, quit approximately 20 years ago

MEDICATIONS (Current)
- Savolitinib 300 mg orally once daily (trial drug)
- Osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg orally once daily (trial drug)
- Furosemide 20 mg orally once daily (savolitinib-related peripheral edema)
- Folic acid 400 mcg orally once daily (residual from pemetrexed protocol; to be discontinued per discussion below)
- Topical clindamycin 1% solution to affected facial areas PRN
- Urea-based moisturizer twice daily
- Acetaminophen 500 mg PRN (occasional use)
- Ondansetron 8 mg orally PRN nausea (infrequent use)

ALLERGIES: No known drug allergies.

SOCIAL HISTORY: Born in Hong Kong, immigrated to the United States at age 32. High school math teacher, currently working part-time (3 days/week). Married, lives with family. Former smoker (12 pack-years, quit ~20 years ago). Denies alcohol. Denies illicit drug use. Good social support network.

FAMILY HISTORY: Paternal uncle -- lung cancer (type not specified). No other known hereditary cancer syndrome.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vital Signs:
  Blood pressure: 118/72 mmHg
  Heart rate: 74 bpm, regular
  Respiratory rate: 16 breaths/min
  Temperature: 36.8 degrees C
  SpO2: 97% on room air
  Weight: [current, stable per patient report]
  Height: 158 cm
  BSA: approximately 1.55 m2
  ECOG Performance Status: 1

General: Well-appearing Asian woman, alert and oriented x4, in no acute distress. Appears her stated age. Neatly dressed; she came with her adult daughter.

Skin/Integument: Grade 1 papulopustular rash over forehead and nasal bridge, without crusting or superinfection. No hand-foot syndrome. Periungual regions intact bilaterally; prior right index finger paronychia resolved. No concerning new skin lesions.

Head, Eyes, Ears, Nose, Throat: Atraumatic, normocephalic. Pupils equal and reactive to light bilaterally, extra-ocular movements intact. No papilledema on fundoscopic exam. Oropharynx clear; no mucositis. Dentition intact. No trismus.

Neck/Lymph Nodes: No palpable cervical lymphadenopathy. Right supraclavicular region: No palpable lymphadenopathy (previously palpable at diagnosis, now resolved). Left supraclavicular: negative. No axillary adenopathy.

Chest/Pulmonary: Thorax symmetric, no deformity. Respiratory effort unlabored. Percussion unremarkable. On auscultation: mildly diminished breath sounds at the right apex, stable from prior exams -- consistent with known parenchymal changes from primary tumor and treatment effect. No crackles, no wheeze, no pleural rub.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, no rubs, no gallops. No JVD.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly, no splenomegaly. No palpable masses. Active bowel sounds.

Extremities: Bilateral lower extremity edema, 1+ pitting to mid-ankle (improved from prior grade 2 to mid-shin). No erythema, no warmth, no calf tenderness to palpation. Peripheral pulses 2+ bilaterally.

Neurological: Alert and oriented x4. Speech fluent, no word-finding difficulty detected on casual conversation or on directed naming task (three-item recall intact, clock draw normal). Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 5/5 in all four extremities. Coordination intact (no finger-nose-finger ataxia, no dysdiadochokinesia). Gait steady without assistance. Romberg negative. No focal neurological deficit.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
(Most recent, obtained within 72 hours of today's visit)

Complete Blood Count:
  WBC: 5.8 x 10^9/L (normal)
  ANC: 3.2 x 10^9/L (normal)
  Hemoglobin: 11.4 g/dL (mild normocytic anemia, stable)
  Platelets: 187 x 10^9/L (normal)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
  Sodium: 138 mEq/L
  Potassium: 3.7 mEq/L
  Bicarbonate: 24 mEq/L
  BUN: 14 mg/dL
  Creatinine: 0.72 mg/dL (eGFR >60, stable renal function)
  Glucose: 96 mg/dL
  Calcium: 9.1 mg/dL
  Total bilirubin: 0.6 mg/dL
  ALT: 22 U/L (normal)
  AST: 18 U/L (normal)
  Alkaline phosphatase: 68 U/L (normal)
  Albumin: 3.8 g/dL

Coagulation: PT/INR within normal limits.

Magnesium: 1.9 mEq/L (within normal range; monitored given diuretic use).

Protocol-Specified Correlative Biomarker:
  ctDNA (plasma): EGFR L858R allele frequency declining trend (most recent submitted; prior result 0.3% AF at 8-week restaging). MET amplification below detection threshold in plasma -- maintained from prior assessment.

Cardiac: Most recent EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, QTc 432 ms (within acceptable range for osimertinib monitoring).

---

IMAGING RESULTS
(Protocol-specified 6-month on-trial restaging; reviewed in detail prior to visit)

CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis with Contrast:
  Right upper lobe: Residual mass at the prior primary tumor site measures 1.3 cm in longest dimension, essentially unchanged from the 8-week restaging measurement of 1.4 cm. This residual opacity has been stable for several months, and its contours are smooth, with surrounding parenchymal scarring/architectural distortion consistent with treatment effect (fibrosis vs. minimal residual viable tumor -- radiologically indeterminate). No evidence of interval enlargement. Pleural surfaces intact, no effusion. No new pulmonary nodules.
  Mediastinum and Hila: No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes. The right paratracheal region and right hilar stations remain normal. The right supraclavicular region is without adenopathy radiologically.
  Abdomen/Pelvis: Liver: No focal hepatic lesions. Adrenals: Bilateral adrenal glands appear normal, no suspicious masses. Kidneys: No suspicious lesions. Pelvis: No pelvic masses or lymphadenopathy.
  Osseous Structures (as imaged): No lytic or blastic lesions appreciated on the imaged spine or visualized ribs.
  Overall chest impression: Continued partial response/stable disease. The dominant mass has shown no meaningful growth from the prior scan and remains dramatically reduced from the diagnostic baseline of 5.8 cm. No new intrathoracic or extrathoracic disease.

MRI Brain with and without Gadolinium:
  Left frontal lesion: Previously 12 mm at the time of progression, 7 mm at 3-month post-SRS, 4 mm at 8-week trial restaging -- now measures approximately 2 mm, essentially approaching radiologic resolution. No surrounding vasogenic edema. No abnormal enhancement.
  Right cerebellar hemisphere lesion: Stable at approximately 4 mm, with minimal to no surrounding signal change. Stable from prior.
  Left parietal lesion: Not visualized on today's scan (was 3 mm at 3-month post-SRS restaging, not visualized at 8-week trial restaging -- consistent with complete radiologic response at this site).
  No new enhancing lesions identified elsewhere in the brain. No leptomeningeal enhancement. Ventricles normal in caliber. No midline shift. The pre-existing scattered periventricular T2/FLAIR hyperintensities consistent with chronic small vessel ischemic change are unchanged.
  Overall brain impression: Very favorable intracranial response -- two of three original metastases are either no longer visible or approaching resolution; the right cerebellar lesion is stable without growth. No new intracranial disease.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Margaret Yuen is a 65-year-old woman with Stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma with MET amplification as a dominant acquired resistance mechanism, currently on a Phase II clinical trial of savolitinib plus osimertinib. She presents today at 6 months on trial with the following overall impression:

1. DISEASE STATUS -- ONGOING PARTIAL RESPONSE, STABLE:
Imaging today demonstrates a continued and durable partial response. The right upper lobe residual mass is stable at 1.3 cm -- representing roughly a 78% reduction from her diagnostic baseline of 5.8 cm. The intracranial disease continues to improve: the left frontal lesion has nearly resolved (2 mm), the left parietal lesion is no longer visible, and the right cerebellar lesion is stable. There is no evidence of new disease anywhere. This is a very encouraging result at 6 months on trial, and represents her best overall response maintained over time. The molecular correlative data (ctDNA with declining EGFR L858R signal, MET amplification below threshold in plasma) support ongoing biologic suppression of the dominant resistant clone. I discussed these results with Ms. Yuen and her daughter in detail today. She was visibly relieved and expressed gratitude. She understands that this is a partial response and that residual radiologic abnormality at the primary site may represent a mix of fibrotic/treatment-related change and residual viable disease, and that ongoing monitoring is essential.

2. TOLERABILITY / ADVERSE EVENTS:
Overall, she is tolerating the combination well at 6 months. Active grade 1 toxicities include:
   - Grade 1 acneiform rash (face) -- managed with topical clindamycin and moisturizer, acceptable to patient, no dose modification warranted
   - Grade 1 peripheral edema, bilateral lower extremities -- well controlled on furosemide 20 mg daily, improved from prior grade 2
   - Grade 1 fatigue -- persistent but not limiting function significantly; she is back to part-time teaching
   - Grade 1 intermittent nausea -- managed PRN with ondansetron as needed
   No grade 2 or higher toxicities are active at this time. No hepatotoxicity, no pneumonitis, no significant QTc prolongation. Renal function is stable. Bone marrow function is adequate. The mild normocytic anemia (Hgb 11.4) has been stable and is felt to reflect residual treatment-related effect from prior chemotherapy; no intervention planned.
   I reviewed the protocol toxicity grading criteria and adverse event reporting requirements with the patient. She is instructed to call the clinic or on-call service for any new or worsening symptoms, and in particular for any new neurological symptoms, significant dyspnea, chest pain, rash worsening, or fever.

3. MEDICATION MANAGEMENT:
   - Savolitinib 300 mg daily: Continue at current dose. No dose modification indicated.
   - Osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily: Continue at current dose. No dose modification indicated.
   - Furosemide 20 mg daily: Continue given persistent grade 1 edema; electrolytes are stable.
   - Folic acid: Given that pemetrexed was discontinued several months ago, folic acid supplementation is no longer required for antifolate protection. Discussed with patient; will discontinue folic acid.
   - Continue topical rash management regimen.
   - Ondansetron as needed.

4. NEUROLOGICAL FOLLOW-UP:
Neurologically, she remains intact today. The SRS-treated lesions are continuing to respond favorably. No dose re-irradiation or neurosurgical consultation is needed at this time. She has no residual neurological deficits -- word-finding difficulty (present at progression) has fully resolved. I explained to her and her daughter that we continue to watch the right cerebellar lesion closely, as it has been stable rather than reducing; if it were to grow, we would reassess with radiation oncology. The small vessel ischemic disease is a pre-existing finding unrelated to her cancer and requires no specific oncologic management.

5. PROTOCOL COMPLIANCE AND CORRELATIVE SAMPLES:
The patient has been compliant with all protocol requirements. Correlative blood samples (ctDNA, PBMC) collected today per protocol schedule. All required documentation completed. No protocol deviations to report.

6. QUALITY OF LIFE AND SUPPORTIVE CARE:
Ms. Yuen has returned to part-time teaching, which is meaningful to her. Her functional status remains ECOG 1. We discussed supportive care optimization. Nutritional counseling referral to be placed given mild persistent appetite decrease, though weight is stable. She has been connected with our thoracic oncology social worker. Psychological support is available; she reports she is coping well overall, with strong family support. We discussed the importance of fall precautions given prior neurological issues (now resolved) and ongoing mild fatigue.

7. PLAN / NEXT STEPS:
   - Continue savolitinib + osimertinib per protocol
   - Discontinue folic acid
   - Continue furosemide 20 mg daily; recheck BMP at next visit
   - Next protocol restaging visit in approximately 8 weeks with repeat CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain
   - ctDNA collection to continue per protocol schedule
   - Nutritional counseling referral placed today
   - Patient to call if any new symptoms, neurological changes, skin toxicity worsening, or other concerns
   - Return to clinic in 4 weeks for interim lab check and symptom assessment; earlier if any acute concerns

Cancer status: Present (residual disease, ongoing partial response on Phase II clinical trial). No new sites of disease. Clinically stable.

Time with patient: Approximately 50 minutes, including review of imaging, detailed discussion of trial status, toxicity review, and Q&A with patient and her daughter.

Patient verbalized understanding of plan. All questions answered.

[Electronically signed, Attending Thoracic Medical Oncologist]
